items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.q: What is the first step of formal analysis?
items.correct: Description - noting what is actually present in the work.
items.why: Students jump to meaning immediately. Formal analysis follows a sequence: describe, analyse formal elements, interpret, evaluate. Description grounds interpretation in what's actually there.

items.correct: Strong contrast between light and dark to model three-dimensional form.
items.why: Students think it's about subject matter (night scenes). Chiaroscuro is a TECHNIQUE using value contrast to suggest volume - exemplified by Caravaggio but used since the Renaissance to model form.
It's Van Gogh. “Monet” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Linseed oil. “Water” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximate century when oil painting became dominant in Europe?
items.why: The real figure is 15 century. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
